summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a legendary 1974 Boston show, highlighted by a smoking Half Step > Roses > Jack Straw sequence and superb jams throughout.
review: The recording is praised as crystal‑clear and well‑balanced, with no technical complaints from the reviewers. Musically the concert shines – Jerry’s vocals are described as “sweet,” the band delivers standout performances of Half Step → Roses → Jack Straw, Sugar Magnolia/Scarlet Begonias, Weather Report Suite, and a rare encore of Ship of Fools. The jam after the WRS is singled out as one of the best 30‑minute stretches ever captured.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviewers are enthusiastic, give high star ratings and highlight numerous exceptional song performances while noting a flawless soundboard recording.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, balanced mix with no reported drop‑outs or muddiness; reviewers repeatedly call the audio “really GOOD” and “crisp.”

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers note a seamless flow, especially the energetic first set sequence and an epic jam after the Weather Report Suite; one quibble about the US Blues → Promised Land link does not detract from the overall momentum.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals described as sweet and mighty fine; his guitar tone praised throughout.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s playing noted as sounding like a xylophone during the jam, highlighting his tonal texture.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey is hinted at possibly playing a xylophone‑like part at 8:18, emphasizing an adventurous percussive moment.
